title: Golden Crisp German Jerusalem Artichoke Gratin
description: A winey, crisp, and cozy German dish featuring Jerusalem artichokes, this gratin is perfect for a cold winter night, and is also FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, combine the sliced Jerusalem artichokes and enough cold water to cover them, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the artichokes are slightly tender.
Drain the artichokes and set them aside.
In the same saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat, then add the olive oil, sliced onion, and garlic-infused oil, and cook until the onion is softened.
Add the white wine and chicken broth to the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Add the cooked Jerusalem artichokes to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Transfer the artichoke mixture to a slate-tile vessel, then top with the shredded gruyere cheese.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley, salt, and black pepper over the top of the cheese.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the artichokes are tender.
